title: Unable to resolve corporate resources from containers by DNS names (WSL)
body:
### Issue Description

I'm unable to access corporate resources (via VPN) by DNS names from Podman containers running under the WSL.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Corporate resources are accessible by their names from the Fedora VM:

```
C:\Users\60131474>podman machine stop
Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully

C:\Users\60131474>podman machine set --rootful

C:\Users\60131474>podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
API forwarding listening on: npipe:////./pipe/docker_engine

Docker API clients default to this address. You do not need to set DOCKER_HOST.
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully

C:\Users\60131474>podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:61307' (ECDSA) to the list of known hosts.
Last login: Wed Jun 21 13:33:53 2023 from ::1
Last login: Wed Jun 21 13:33:53 2023 from ::1

[root@HQ6193 ~]# ping resource.corp.com
PING resource.corp.com (10.80.50.151) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from resource.corp.com (10.80.50.151): icmp_seq=1 ttl=61 time=74.1 ms
64 bytes from resource.corp.com (10.80.50.151): icmp_seq=2 ttl=61 time=153 ms
^C
--- resource.corp.com ping statistics ---
2 packets transmitted, 2 received, 0% packet loss, time 1002ms
rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 74.051/113.325/152.599/39.274 ms
```

At the same time, their names are not available from containers:
```
[root@HQ6193 ~]# podman run --rm alpine ping resource.corp.com
ping: bad address 'resource.corp.com'
```

They are only accessible by IPs:
```
[root@HQ6193 ~]# podman run --rm alpine ping 10.80.50.151
PING 10.80.50.151 (10.80.50.151): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=0 ttl=42 time=93.132 ms
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=1 ttl=42 time=98.175 ms
^C
--- 10.80.50.151 ping statistics ---
2 packets transmitted, 2 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 93.132/95.653/98.175 ms
```

Other public resources are accessible by their DNS names:
```
[root@HQ6193 ~]# podman run --rm alpine ping google.com
PING google.com (173.194.222.100): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 173.194.222.100: seq=0 ttl=42 time=106.423 ms
^C
--- google.com ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 106.423/106.423/106.423 ms
```

I tried to set up a custom network as suggested in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17075. But without success:
```
[root@HQ6193 ~]# podman network create new
new
[root@HQ6193 ~]# podman run --rm --network new alpine ping resource.corp.com
ping: bad address 'resource.corp.com'
```

### Describe the results you received

Unable to resolve corporate resources names. Public resources are reachable by DNS names.

### Describe the results you expected

I'm able to reach all the resources available for the host machine from containers by their DNS names.

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

`resolv.conf` of the VM:
```
[root@HQ6193 ~]# cat /etc/resolv.conf
# This file was automatically generated by WSL. To stop automatic generation of this file, add the following entry to /etc/wsl.conf:
# [network]
# generateResolvConf = false
nameserver 172.17.192.1
```

`resolv.conf` of containers:
```
[root@HQ6193 ~]# podman run --rm alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf
nameserver 172.17.192.1
```

### Additional information

Once going through the [The container name resolution conundrum](https://blog.podman.io/2023/02/the-container-name-resolution-conundrum/) I was able to get ping fully working from the container. But later on was not able to reproduce, even using the same sequence of commands.

comments.body:
I've just got this unstable behaviour again:

```
C:\Users\60131474>podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:61307' (ECDSA) to the list of known hosts.
Last login: Wed Jun 21 15:39:25 2023 from ::1
Last login: Wed Jun 21 15:39:25 2023 from ::1

[root@HQ6193 ~]# podman run --rm -it --network=host alpine
/ # ping resource.corp.com
PING resource.corp.com (10.80.50.151): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=0 ttl=42 time=91.478 ms
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=1 ttl=42 time=128.645 ms
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=2 ttl=42 time=135.693 ms
^C
--- resource.corp.com ping statistics ---
3 packets transmitted, 3 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 91.478/118.605/135.693 ms
/ # exit

[root@HQ6193 ~]# podman run --rm -it --network=host alpine
/ # ping resource.corp.com
ping: bad address 'resource.corp.com'
```

There was a slight pause before the `podman machine ssh` output appeared.

Moreover. It breaks while container is running:
```
[root@HQ6193 ~]# podman run --rm -it --privileged alpine

/ # ping resource.corp.com
PING resource.corp.com (10.80.50.151): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=0 ttl=60 time=91.734 ms
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=1 ttl=60 time=89.001 ms
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=2 ttl=60 time=111.726 ms
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=3 ttl=60 time=87.497 ms
64 bytes from 10.80.50.151: seq=4 ttl=60 time=88.791 ms
^C
--- resource.corp.com ping statistics ---
5 packets transmitted, 5 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 87.497/93.749/111.726 ms

/ # ping resource.corp.com
ping: bad address 'resource.corp.com'
```
Both commands were run one after another, without pause.

> Both commands were run one after another, without pause.

I was seeing unpredictable intermittent successes in resolution identical to the comment above, however as of today resolution has entirely stopped working and is no longer intermittent.

```
PS C:\> nslookup resource.internaldomain.com
Server:  UnKnown
Address:  10.39.5.5

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:    resource.internaldomain.com
Address:  10.108.251.8

PS C:\> ping resource.internaldomain.com

Pinging resource.internaldomain.com [10.108.251.8] with 32 bytes of data:
Reply from 10.108.251.8: bytes=32 time=43ms TTL=127
Reply from 10.108.251.8: bytes=32 time=32ms TTL=127
Reply from 10.108.251.8: bytes=32 time=32ms TTL=127
Reply from 10.108.251.8: bytes=32 time=32ms TTL=127

Ping statistics for 10.108.251.8:
    Packets: Sent = 4, Received = 4, Lost = 0 (0% loss),
Approximate round trip times in milli-seconds:
    Minimum = 32ms, Maximum = 43ms, Average = 34ms
PS C:\> podman run --rm -it --privileged alpine
/ # nslookup resource.internaldomain.com
Server:         172.20.224.1
Address:        172.20.224.1:53

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   resource.internaldomain.com
Address: 10.108.251.8

** server can't find resource.internaldomain.com: NXDOMAIN

/ # ping resource.internaldomain.com
ping: bad address 'resource.internaldomain.com'
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Are you still seeing this?  I know that Windows has some issues with VPNs coming up and going down.
@n1hility thoughts?

We recently added a --user-mode-networking feature in 4.6 to support setups where a VPN drops vm traffic. It does require that you are on a recent version of WSL (wsl --version returns version numbers and not help text. If you see help text you need to run windows updates and then wsl --update)

To use it recreate your podman machine (after backing up any data you wish to keep) like so:

```
podman machine rm
podman machine init --user-mode-networking
```
